entities.name: dharma
entities.cite: those paths of dharma that stand unbroken

entities.name: tarka
entities.cite: tarka arrives too late to sit in judgement

axioms.name: lokaprasiddha
axioms.cite: their being established in the world, functions as the warrant

denials.name: badhate
denials.cite: Reasoning does not get to arraign an unbroken practice

rejected_readings.label: tarka_overturns
rejected_readings.rendering: Reasoning can overturn the unbroken paths of dharma.
rejected_readings.why: Directly contradicts the verse's denial that tarka badhate dharma. The verse explicitly says no one sets them aside by reasoning.

rejected_readings.label: tarka_ground_dharma
rejected_readings.rendering: Reasoning is what establishes and grounds the dharmic paths in the world.
rejected_readings.why: Inverts the epistemological priority. The verse insists that dharma's establishment in the world is prior and independent of reasoning; tarka arrives too late to judge. This reading incorrectly makes reasoning the ground of dharma's worldly establishment.

notes: The contract captures the asymmetry with 1.30: reasoning cannot establish dharma (1.30) and cannot overturn it (1.31). The verse's structure is: unbroken paths + worldly establishment = invulnerability to reasoning. This clarifies reasoning's scope rather than negating its validity.
